studies.title: My7steps App Pilot Study
studies.brief_summary:
The purpose of the "My7steps App Pilot Study" is to test the effectiveness of the "My7steps" health app, which was developed for depressive symptoms.

The app aims to simplify access to mental health care and reduce waiting times.

In the treatment, which lasts up to four weeks, psychotherapeutic principles are implemented digitally. Before and after the treatment, the study participants will be questioned in personal interviews. The interviews are conducted in German, English, Arabic or Dari/Farsi.

Patients in standard German care who are either waiting for a place in therapy or who want to try out the app as an alternative to clinical offers in the healthcare system can take part. The participation offer is aimed at people who are 18 years of age or older and suffer from depressive symptoms or have to cope with stressful life events.

classification.reason: The study does not explicitly mention randomization, control groups, or a prospective interventional design. It appears to be an observational pilot study testing the app in a real-world setting with patients in standard care. The lack of clear RCT indicators like 'randomized' or 'placebo-controlled' leans towards RWE.
